revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 2 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 2-ngie̍t]][[ja:2月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 2월]][[th:เดือนยี่]][[wuu:农历二月]][[za:Ndwenngeih]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 2 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:二月]][[zh:农历二月]]
{{Lunisolar February|qq=}} '''Lunisolar February''' (旧暦二月) is the second {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}). The month that includes {{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|qid=Q1339268|lt=Vernal Equinox|1=Chunfen}} becomes February. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|February|en|February|qid=Q109|lt=February|1=February}} to early {{ill|April|en|April|qid=Q118|lt=April|1=April}}.
Another name for February is '''Kisaragi''' (如月). The etymology is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have February ending on the 29th, with no February 30th. Additionally, in the {{ill|Ryukyuan Dialect|ja|琉球方言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukyuan Dialect|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琉球諸語|1=Ryukyuan languages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56393}}, the period from February in the Lunisolar until the onset of the rainy season in the {{ill|Okinawa|ja|沖縄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 沖縄県}} region is called “Urizun” (meaning "beginning of moisture") . ==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|February (old calendar)|ja|2月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar February|lt_ja=旧暦2月|1=February (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/February (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Kisaragi''' (きさらぎ; can also be written 絹更月 or 衣更月), and today it is also used as an alternate name for February of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}. The term “Kisaragi” simply borrows the Chinese alternate name for the second month and has no direct relation to the Japanese pronunciation “kisaragi.” Various theories exist regarding the origin of the name: * Because in lunar February the cold still remains, it is the month to wear extra garments, hence “Kisaragi” (衣更着). * Because vegetation buds push out, hence “Kusakiharizuki” (草木張月). * Because wild geese arrive in the previous lunar August and, additionally, swallows arrive around this time, hence “Kisaragi” (来更来) with {{ill|Goose|en|Goose|qid=Q16529344|lt=wild goose|1=Goose}} and {{ill|Barn swallow|en|Barn swallow|qid=Q25429|lt=燕|1=Barn swallow}}. * Because warm weather further arrives, hence “Kisaragi” (気更来).
Other alternate names include “Umemizuki” (梅見月) and “Konomezuki” (木の芽月). Lunar February corresponds roughly to March of the Gregorian calendar, when {{ill|plum blossom|ja|梅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plum blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウメ|1=Prunus mume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q157763}} bloom.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, February is called “Tashkur Chup” (タㇱクㇽチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: taskur cup), meaning “month of cold snaps”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
== Dates in Kisaragi == {| class="wikitable" |+ Dates in Kisaragi ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || {{ill|March 1|en|March 1|qid=Q2393|lt=March 1|1=March 1}} || {{ill|March 30|en|March 30|qid=Q2460|lt=March 30|1=March 30}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || {{ill|March 20|en|March 20|qid=Q2427|lt=March 20|1=March 20}} || {{ill|April 18|en|April 18|qid=Q2521|lt=April 18|1=April 18}} || 30 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|March 9|en|March 9|qid=Q2401|lt=March 9|1=March 9}} || {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|qid=Q2506|lt=April 6|1=April 6}} || 29 days |- | 2017 || {{ill|February 26|en|February 26|qid=Q2359|lt=February 26|1=February 26}} || {{ill|March 27|en|March 27|qid=Q2457|lt=March 27|1=March 27}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || {{ill|March 17|en|March 17|qid=Q2419|lt=March 17|1=March 17}} || {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|qid=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|March 7|en|March 7|qid=Q2394|lt=March 7|1=March 7}} || {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|qid=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}} || 29 days |- | 2020 || {{ill|February 24|en|February 24|qid=Q2355|lt=February 24|1=February 24}} || {{ill|March 23|en|March 23|qid=Q2445|lt=March 23|1=March 23}} || 29 days |- | 2021 || {{ill|March 13|en|March 13|qid=Q2400|lt=March 13|1=March 13}} || {{ill|April 11|en|April 11|qid=Q2501|lt=April 11|1=April 11}} || 30 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|March 3|en|March 3|qid=Q2391|lt=March 3|1=March 3}} || {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31|1=March 31}} || 29 days |- | 2023 || {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|qid=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}} || {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|qid=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}} || 30 days |- | 2023 ([[:en:Leap month|leap]]) || {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|qid=Q2435|lt=March 22|1=March 22}} || {{ill|April 19|en|April 19|qid=Q2523|lt=April 19|1=April 19}} || 29 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|March 10|en|March 10|qid=Q2397|lt=March 10|1=March 10}} || {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|qid=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}} || 30 days |- | 2025 || {{ill|February 28|en|February 28|qid=Q2362|lt=February 28|1=February 28}} || {{ill|March 28|en|March 28|qid=Q2458|lt=March 28|1=March 28}} || 29 days |} == Related Articles == {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar February|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|2|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|February 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|2月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
